title: Accountability for Terrorist Perpetrators of October 7th Act
short_title: Accountability for Terrorist Perpetrators of October 7th Act
plain_summary: This law makes the President put special punishments called sanctions on a terrorist group called the Popular Resistance Committees and other similar groups within 90 days. The sanctions will freeze their money and stop them from coming into the United States. The law also makes the Secretary of State write reports to Congress about whether certain terrorist groups should get official terrorist labels. The Secretary of State must keep writing reports every two years about any new terrorist groups that show up.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Accountability for Terrorist Perpetrators of October 7th Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Imposition of Terrorism Sanctions with Respect to the Popular Resistance Committees
sections.text: Congress has found several important facts. Since the year two thousand, the Popular Resistance Committees has carried out many terrorist attacks against people from Israel, America, and Palestine. This group includes former members from Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad, and the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine. The United States has already labeled all of these groups as foreign terrorist organizations. In two thousand three, the Popular Resistance Committees attacked a US diplomatic convoy, injuring one diplomat and killing three American security guards. In two thousand four, they murdered a pregnant Israeli woman and her four daughters who were eleven, nine, seven, and two years old. They have also killed Palestinians, including a former Palestinian Authority security chief in Gaza. Since at least two thousand eleven, the Popular Resistance Committees has been the third largest terrorist group in Gaza, after Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad. On October seventh, two thousand twenty three, Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad, and the Popular Resistance Committees launched a massive attack on Israel by air, land, and sea. They fired thousands of rockets, killed one thousand two hundred innocent people including Israelis and Americans, kidnapped two hundred fifty hostages, and committed widespread torture and sexual violence. The Popular Resistance Committees publicly claimed responsibility for participating in this attack alongside Hamas. They said they kidnapped Israeli soldiers and posted photos of items taken from soldiers they killed and captured. The next day, they claimed responsibility for sending explosive drones during the attack. Starting ninety days after this law takes effect, the President must impose sanctions on the Popular Resistance Committees, any foreign person who is an official or works for them, any foreign person owned or controlled by them, and any current or future armed group the President decides is operating under the Popular Resistance Committees. The sanctions include blocking all property and money belonging to these people or groups if it is in the United States or controlled by Americans. The sanctions also mean these people cannot get visas to enter the United States, cannot be admitted into the country, and any visas they already have will be canceled immediately. There are two exceptions to these sanctions. First, if letting someone into the United States is required by our agreement with the United Nations or other international obligations. Second, the sanctions do not apply to authorized intelligence, law enforcement, or national security activities by the United States. Anyone who violates these sanctions will face the same penalties as those who break the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. The President can use all the powers given under that law to carry out this section. The President can temporarily stop these sanctions for periods of up to one hundred eighty days if he tells Congress at least fifteen days beforehand that doing so is vital to national security. The President can end the sanctions permanently if he tells Congress that the person or group is no longer involved in terrorism or if the organization has shut down.

sections.title: Section 3  Report on the Designation of the Lions' Den and the Popular Resistance Committees as Specially Designated Global Terrorists
sections.text: Congress has found that since Lions' Den was founded in two thousand twenty two, it has carried out many terrorist attacks including more than sixty one shooting attacks against Israeli soldiers and civilians. Like the Popular Resistance Committees, Lions' Den includes former members from Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad, and the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine. Lions' Den receives money from Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad, which are both already designated as foreign terrorist organizations. Within ninety days of this law taking effect, the Secretary of State must give Congress a detailed report. The report must say whether Lions' Den meets the requirements to be labeled as a specially designated global terrorist under Executive Order thirteen thousand two hundred twenty four and as a foreign terrorist organization under immigration law. It must also say whether the Popular Resistance Committees meets the requirements to be labeled as a specially designated global terrorist. If the Secretary decides any of these groups do not meet the requirements, the report must explain in detail which requirements they failed to meet. The report should be unclassified but can include a classified section. Starting one year after this law takes effect, and then every two years after that, the Secretary of State must give Congress another report. This ongoing report must identify any new groups operating under the Popular Resistance Committees or any group that replaces it. For each new group, the report must decide whether it meets the requirements to be designated as a specially designated global terrorist, a foreign terrorist organization, or to have sanctions imposed under section two of this law. If the Secretary identifies a new group but decides it does not meet these requirements, the report must explain in detail why not.

sections.title: Section 4  Definitions
sections.text: In this law, several terms have specific meanings. The terms admitted, alien, and lawfully admitted for permanent residence have the same meanings as in section one hundred one of the Immigration and Nationality Act. Appropriate committees of Congress means the Committee on Foreign Relations and the Committee on the Judiciary in the Senate, and the Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Committee on the Judiciary in the House of Representatives. Entity means a partnership, association, corporation, or other organization, group, or subgroup. Foreign person means an individual or entity that is not a United States person, but does not include the government of a foreign country. Foreign terrorist organization means an entity that has been designated as such under section two hundred nineteen of the Immigration and Nationality Act. United States person means a US citizen, a permanent resident alien, an entity organized under US laws including foreign branches of such entities, or any person in the United States.
